title_original: Before quantum computing arrives, this startup wants enterprises already running on it
summary_original: Finnish entrepreneur Peter Sarlin, after selling Silo AI to AMD for $665 million, has launched Qutwo, an AI company positioning enterprises for the quantum computing era. Funded by Sarlin’s family office PostScriptum, Qutwo’s Qutwo OS serves as an orchestration layer, allowing businesses to seamlessly transition from classical to quantum and hybrid computing. The company is collaborating with clients like Zalando and OP Pohjola, focusing on “large design partnerships which are in the tens of millions.” Qutwo’s team includes quantum experts like IQM co-founder Kuan Yen Tan and Antti Vasara, alongside business leaders such as Pekka Lundmark, former CEO of Nokia. Qutwo emphasizes present-day quantum-inspired computing while preparing for future quantum hardware advances.
